items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` **Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` **Triggered by:** PR #57780 by `@nick863` ## What Changed PR #57780 added the new `AgentToolboxes` client class with full CRUD operations (create, get, list, update, delete) for Agent Toolboxes. A sample test file (`Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`) and a standalone sample markdown (`samples/Sample_Toolboxes_CRUD.md`) were added. The README was updated with a Toolboxes section, but it is incomplete and has pre-existing errors that need correction. ## Gaps Found - **Wrong package name in "Install the package" section**: The README shows `dotnet add package Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI --prerelease` — this is the wrong package. It must be `dotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease`. - **Incorrect boilerplate in "Install the package" section**: The note below the install command mentions "Cosmos DB account (SQL API)" and "C# 8.0 syntax" — this is leftover boilerplate from a different package template and is irrelevant to Azure AI Agents. - **Table of Contents / section heading mismatch**: The Table of Contents lists "Prompt Agents" but the actual heading in the Examples section is "## Declarative Agents". The ToC anchor `#prompt-agents` will not resolve. - **Incomplete Toolboxes section in README**: The `### Toolboxes` subsection only demonstrates creating, getting a ToolboxRecord, and getting a ToolboxVersion. It is missing snippet examples for: updating the default version (`UpdateToolbox`), listing all versions (`GetToolboxVersions`), listing all toolboxes (`GetToolboxes`), deleting a specific version (`DeleteToolboxVersion`), and deleting all versions (`DeleteToolbox`). The sample `.cs` file already has `#region Snippet:` blocks for all of these. - **No Key Concepts entry for `AgentToolboxes`**: The `## Key concepts` section has no explanation of what Toolboxes are, that `AgentToolboxes` is obtained via `agentsClient.GetAgentToolboxes()`, or that this feature requires the `Toolboxes=V1Preview` experimental header.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` — **Edit** **Fix 1 — Wrong package name and boilerplate.** Replace the entire "Install the package" subsection: ```markdown ### Install the package Install the client library for .NET with [NuGet]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ```shell dotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease ``` ``` Remove the incorrect note that follows about "Azure subscription and Cosmos DB account (SQL API)" and "C# 8.0 syntax". --- **Fix 2 — Table of Contents anchor mismatch.** In the Table of Contents, change: ```markdown - [Prompt Agents](#prompt-agents) ``` to: ```markdown - [Declarative Agents](#declarative-agents) ``` --- **Fix 3 — Key Concepts: add Toolboxes concept.** In the `## Key concepts` section (after the existing `### Service API versions` subsection), add a new subsection: ```markdown ### Agent Toolboxes `AgentToolboxes` is a sub-client used to store and manage collections of tools in Azure so that Agents can retrieve and use them. A **toolbox** may have multiple versions; each version holds a list of `ProjectsAgentTool` objects. A `ToolboxRecord` represents a toolbox by name and tracks its default version, while `ToolboxVersion` represents a specific version with its full tool list. `AgentToolboxes` is obtained from `AgentAdministrationClient`: ```C# Snippet:Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD ``` > **Note:** This feature requires the `Toolboxes=V1Preview` experimental header and has the `[Experimental("AAIP001")]` attribute. Suppress the `AAIP001` diagnostic before using these APIs. ``` --- **Fix 4 — Incomplete Toolboxes section.** In the `### Toolboxes` subsection under `## Examples`, extend the section to include the missing operations. After the existing snippets for GetToolbox and GetToolboxVersion, add: ```markdown To update the default version of a toolbox, use `UpdateToolboxAsync`: ```C# Snippet:Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` To list all versions of a toolbox, use `GetToolboxVersionsAsync`: ```C# Snippet:Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` To list all toolboxes, use `GetToolboxesAsync`: ```C# Snippet:Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` To delete toolboxes, use `DeleteToolboxVersionAsync` to remove a specific version, or `DeleteToolboxAsync` to remove all versions. Note that the current default version cannot be deleted directly; change the default version first. ```C# Snippet:Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets All required `#region Snippet:` regions are already present in the existing sample test file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs` The following snippet names exist in that file and need corresponding placeholder fences added to README.md (as described in Step 1, Fix 4 above): - `Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async` - `Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async` - `Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async` - `Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async` For the Key Concepts snippet (Fix 3): - `Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD` — already has a `#region` block in the test file; the `#if SNIPPET` guard hides the test-specific lines correctly. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` currently contains all required top-level sections. No sections are missing, but the following need correction: 1. **Getting started > Install the package** — wrong package name and irrelevant boilerplate (described in Fix 1) 2. **Table of contents** — anchor mismatch for "Prompt Agents" vs "Declarative Agents" (described in Fix 2) 3. **Key concepts** — missing `AgentToolboxes` concept entry (described in Fix 3) 4. **Examples > Toolboxes** — incomplete; missing update, list, and delete snippet examples (described in Fix 4) Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly with injected snippet content 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — no public API changes are expected for this documentation-only fix; run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title:
